    "correctOptionIndex": 0,
    "marks": 1,
    "difficulty": "Moderate",
    "topic": "Probability",
    "blooms": "Analyze",
    "solutionSteps": [
      "Step 1: Define events $E_1, E_2, E_3$ for choosing the three coins ($P(E_i) = 1/3$) and $H$ for getting a head.",
      "Step 2: Identify conditional probabilities: $P(H|E_1) = 1$, $P(H|E_2) = 3/4$, $P(H|E_3) = 1/2$.",
      "Step 3: Use Bayes' Theorem: $P(E_1|H) = \\\\frac{P(E_1)P(H|E_1)}{\\\\sum P(E_i)P(H|E_i)} = \\\\frac{1/3 \\\\cdot 1}{1/3(1 + 3/4 + 1/2)} = \\\\frac{1}{9/4} = 4/9$."
